info.title: Nexo Payment Gateway

tags.name: Deposit Addresses (v1)
tags.description:
Operations concerning the creation and management of deposit addresses. A
  deposit address provides a blockchain address, tailored to facilitate
  distinct transactions per entity via a unique `referenceId`.
 
  Workflow:
  - One or more deposit addresses are generated based on the provided
  `referenceId`.
  - These addresses are associated with specific blockchain transactions,
  and their creation invokes WebHook events, which notify you at a
  configured URL about transaction activities.
  - A deposit address remains unique to the `referenceId` across blockchains
  of the same family. This means that repeating a `referenceId` in a request
  within the same blockchain family will return the previously generated
  address.
 
  Benefits:
  - **Streamlined Transactions**: Each entity interacts through a unique
  blockchain address, enhancing tracking and management of individual
  transactions.
  - **Consistent Address Reuse**: For blockchains within the same family, a
  single address can be reused for entities across different sessions or
  interactions, reducing the need to generate multiple addresses and
  simplifying the entity's experience.
  - **Notifications**: Updates via WebHooks ensure timely responses to all
  transaction activities related to the deposit addresses.
 
  Restrictions:
  - **Reference Identifier**: Reference Identifier (`referenceId`) is
  versatile, but some basic constraints are placed:
    - Up to 50 Characters in length can be passed.
    - Only Latin characters [aA-zZ], numbers [0-9], dashes (-), backslashes (\), underscore (_).

tags.name: Assets (v1)
tags.description: Operations related to the assets supported by the API, including listing available assets and providing detailed information about each asset's characteristics.

post.description:
Creates a deposit address associated with the provided `referenceId`. 
  This address facilitates the reception of cryptocurrency deposits.
 
  If a deposit address already exists for the same `referenceId` in the same
  blockchain family, the existing address is returned to prevent duplicate
  address generation. This ensures efficient utilization of addresses within
  the same blockchain type, where the same deposit address may be used for
  multiple assets.
  
  Notifications for deposits to this address will be sent to the previously
  configured WebHook URL, with details identifying the specific transaction
  and `referenceId`.
 
  Use this endpoint to streamline the management of deposit addresses,
  reducing the need for multiple address generations, and enhancing tracking
  capabilities.

400.description:
Bad request - This error occurs when the request is improperly formatted or missing required information.
 Refer to the error message for more details.

example.title: Bad Request
example.detail: Merchant not onboarded, or asset not supported

401.description:
Unauthorized - Occurs when the API key provided is invalid, expired, or
 missing in the request headers. Ensure your API key is correct and included in the header.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Indicates an error on the server side. If this error persists, contact Nexo Client Care for resolution.

example.title: Internal Server Error
example.detail: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

get.description: Returns all assets available for the merchant through the Nexo Payment Gateway. This includes metadata such as symbol, blockchain, and other relevant properties.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of supported assets for merchant.

400.description:
Bad request - This error occurs when the request is improperly formatted or missing required information.
 Refer to the error message for more details.

401.description:
Unauthorized - Occurs when the API key provided is invalid, expired, or
 missing in the request headers. Ensure your API key is correct and included in the header.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Indicates an error on the server side. If this error persists, contact Nexo Client Care for resolution.

example.title: Internal Server Error
example.detail: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

api_key.description: The `API Key` value, passed in the x-api-key header
api_key.name: x-api-key

referenceId.description: A unique identifier provided by the merchant used to reference the deposit address.

asset.description: The type of cryptocurrency asset for which the deposit address is being created.

CreateDepositAddressRequest.description: Request payload containing necessary data to create a new deposit address or retrieve an existing one.

referenceId.description: A unique reference identifier generated for the merchant's deposit address.

asset.description: The type of cryptocurrency asset for which the deposit address has been created.

blockchain.description: The blockchain network (Ethereum, Bitcoin, etc.) associated with the asset.

address.description: The blockchain address created for depositing the specified asset.

CreateDepositAddressResponse.description: Successfully created or retrieved an existing deposit address. **Created:** A new deposit address has been generated for the `referenceId`. **Existing:** An existing address matching the `referenceId` and `blockchain type` was found and returned.

field.description: The name of the field involved in the error, if applicable.

detail.description: A detailed message about the error concerning the field.

CustomErrorDetails.description: A list of detailed errors, if multiple issues occurred during the request.

example.detail: Reference ID must be at least 6 characters.

traceId.description: A unique identifier for the request that can be used to trace the error.

type.description: A URI that categorizes the type of error.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the error.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this error.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the error.

instance.description: A URI that uniquely identifies the specific occurrence of the error within the API

errors.description: A list of detailed errors, if multiple issues occurred during the request.

example.detail: Reference ID must be at least 6 characters.

asset.description: The supported asset symbol. (e.g. BTC, ETH, USDC).

blockchain.description: The name of the blockchain network where the asset is deployed (e.g. Ethereum, Bitcoin). Please refer to the list of supported networks.

address.description: The blockchain address of the asset. If the asset is native to the blockchain, this will be an empty string. For non-native tokens, this will be the specific address on the blockchain.

decimals.description: The number of decimals the asset uses to define its smallest unit.
